title: Hylocharis robusta
intro: Hylocharis robusta, commonly known as the Blue-crowned Quail-dove Flower or Robust Violet-eared Hummingbird Flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, making it a promising natural remedy for cardiovascular diseases. The bioactive constituents of Hylocharis robusta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Hylocharis robusta include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including hypertension, fever, and respiratory issues.

The family Trochilidae is a large and diverse group of birds that are found in the Americas. The genus Hylocharis is further divided into several species of hummingbirds, each with unique characteristics. As a member of the family Trochilidae and the genus Hylocharis, Hylocharis robusta plays an important role in its ecosystem as a pollinator and seed disperser.
